ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken skewer food product entry; no exact entry for lime-sriracha marinated variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific lime-sriracha chicken skewer entry. A0CKP (grilled chicken meat) is the closest defensible parent code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 is the closest parent for a prepared chicken dish; no specific skewer or sriracha variant code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of lime and sriracha chicken skewers consists of approximately 3 to 4 skewers totalling around 200 g of marinated grilled chicken.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken breast, grilled / Grilled chicken with marinade — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods, adapted with marinade ingredient adjustments
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for lime-sriracha chicken skewers exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for grilled chicken breast (NDB 05064) with adjustments for sriracha sauce (~5 g per 100 g serving contribution), lime juice (~3 g per 100 g), soy sauce (~4 g per 100 g), and sesame oil (~2 g per 100 g). Sodium is elevated relative to plain grilled chicken due to soy sauce and sriracha. Energy, protein, and fat are consistent with grilled marinated chicken breast at approximately 165–175 kcal per 100 g. Values represent a reasonable mid-range estimate for this preparation style.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — grilled chicken and mixed protein-fat-carbohydrate dishes
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lime-sriracha chicken skewers. GI estimated at 40 based on analogous grilled marinated chicken dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). Grilled chicken with small amounts of sugar-containing marinade typically yields a low GI (35–45) due to dominant protein and fat content suppressing glycemic response. Carbohydrate per 100 g is 5.2 g; GL per 200 g serving = (40 × 10.4) / 100 = 4.2, classified as low GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Elevated sodium from soy sauce and sriracha marinade
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a 200 g serving contributes approximately 1040 mg sodium, representing roughly 45% of the 2300 mg daily limit recommended by the American Heart Association; requesting reduced-soy-sauce preparation or limiting portion size is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and high protein content supporting glycemic control
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 40 and a glycemic load of 4.2 per standard serving, lime and sriracha chicken skewers are suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed as part of a balanced meal; the high protein content (22.5 g per 100 g) further supports satiety and attenuates postprandial glucose rise.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate sodium and saturated fat content; lean protein source overall
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While the lean grilled chicken provides a favourable protein-to-fat ratio and low saturated fat (1.3 g per 100 g), the sodium content from the marinade warrants caution for individuals with cardiovascular disease; choosing a reduced-sodium preparation and pairing with vegetables can improve the overall cardiovascular profile of the meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lime and Sriracha Chicken Skewers are a contemporary fusion dish served in Singapore, consisting of boneless chicken pieces — typically breast or thigh — marinated in a blend of fresh lime juice, sriracha hot sauce, soy sauce, garlic, sesame oil, and a small amount of sugar, then threaded onto skewers and grilled over high heat. The result is a dish with a bright, tangy citrus flavour balanced by the moderate heat and vinegary depth of sriracha, with a slightly caramelised exterior from the sugars in the marinade. The texture is tender and juicy when properly cooked, with light charring on the surface. Nutritionally, the dish is a high-protein, moderate-fat, low-carbohydrate option with approximately 168 kcal per 100 g, 22.5 g protein, 5.8 g fat, and 5.2 g carbohydrate. Sodium is elevated at approximately 520 mg per 100 g due to soy sauce and sriracha, making portion awareness important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 40 (low), making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is halal-friendly and dairy-free, though it contains soy and sesame allergens. It is commonly served as a main course or sharing plate in casual dining and food delivery contexts in Singapore, often accompanied by steamed rice, salad, or dipping sauces.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for lime-sriracha chicken skewers exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional food composition database. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy grilled chicken breast data with marinade ingredient adjustments. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for comparable grilled marinated chicken dishes. Confidence is medium rather than low because the adaptation methodology is transparent, the source data is from an approved primary database, and the marinade ingredients are well-characterised.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy grilled chicken breast source entry for this preparation style. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for grilled chicken breast with plant-based marinade ingredients — no ruminant fat sources present. Cuisine tag in the input JSON listed 'Taiwanese, Burger, Sandwich' which is inconsistent with the dish identity; the dish is a contemporary fusion preparation served in Singapore and has been reclassified accordingly. The restaurant 'Riverside Buns - Boat Quay' is noted as the single source restaurant from field survey data; this is a delivery platform record and nutrient values are not derived from the platform. GI set to 40 as an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement for this specific dish is not available in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the accessed date.
